gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف على المشهور - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (22/218)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف على المشهور - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (22/218)
text: سَمِعْتُ أَبَا الْحَسَنِ ( عليه السلام ) يَقُولُ أَكْلُ الْجَزَرِ يُسَخِّنُ الْكُلْيَتَيْنِ وَ يَنْصِبُ الذَّكَرَ قَالَ فَقُلْتُ لَهُ جُعِلْتُ فِدَاكَ كَيْفَ آكُلُهُ وَ لَيْسَ لِي أَسْنَانٌ قَالَ فَقَالَ لِي مُرِ الْجَارِيَةَ تَسْلُقُهُ وَ كُلْهُ .

en.hubeali: A number of our companions, from Sahl Bin Ziyad, from Ibrahim Bin Abdul Rahman, from his father, from Dawood Bin Farqad who said,

‘I heard Abu Al-Hassan<sup>asws</sup> saying: ‘Eating the carrots warms the kidneys, and establishes the manhood’. I said to him<sup>asws</sup>, ‘May I be sacrificed for you<sup>asws</sup>! How can I eat it and there are no teeth for me’. So he<sup>asws</sup> said to me: ‘Instruct the maid to boil it, and eat it’.
en.sarwar: 3. A number of our people have narrated from Sahl ibn Ziyad from Ibrahim ibn ‘Abd al-Rahman from his father from Dawud ibn Farqad who has said the following: “I once heard abu al-Hassan, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, saying, ’Eating carrot warms up the kidneys and causes erection.’ He (the narrator) has said that he asked, ‘I pray to Allah to keep my soul in service for your cause, how should I eat it when I have no teeth?’ He (the Imam) said, ’Order the girl to boil it, then eat.’”
